name: Phase 3, Randomized, Open-Label Study of Vepdegestrant (ARV-471) Plus Palbociclib vs Letrozole Plus Palbociclib in Participants With ER(+)/HER2(-) Advanced Breast Cancer (VERITAC-3)

titles.text: Phase 3, Randomized, Open-Label Study of Vepdegestrant (ARV-471) Plus Palbociclib vs Letrozole Plus Palbociclib in Participants With ER(+)/HER2(-) Advanced Breast Cancer (VERITAC-3)

A PHASE 3, RANDOMIZED, OPEN-LABEL, MULTI-CENTER STUDY OF VEPDEGESTRANT (ARV-471/PF-07850327) PLUS PALBOCICLIB VERSUS LETROZOLE PLUS PALBOCICLIB FOR THE TREATMENT OF PARTICIPANTS WITH ESTROGEN RECEPTOR-POSITIVE, HER2-NEGATIVE BREAST CANCER WHO HAVE NOT RECEIVED ANY PRIOR SYSTEMIC ANTI-CANCER TREATMENT FOR ADVANCED DISEASE (VERITAC-3)

changes.rationale: To fulfill the obligation to provide study treatment to participants currently on treatment still receiving a benefit from the study intervention

amendments.summary: The main purpose of the amendment is to reduce the Schedule of Activities with clinical assessments per standard of care or clinical judgment of the investigator. This amendment also reflects the previous decision that the Phase 3 portion of the study will not be conducted, due to a Sponsor decision and not for safety or efficacy reasons or requests from Regulatory Agencies; SLI Phase enrollment was completed for the planned participants. In addition, this amendment is to update the end of study definition and the section regarding continued access to study intervention after the end of study.

primaryReason.otherReason: The main purpose of the amendment is to reduce the Schedule of Activities with clinical assessments per standard of care or clinical judgment of the investigator. This amendment also reflects the previous decision that the Phase 3 portion of the study will not be conducted, due to a Sponsor decision and not for safety or efficacy reasons or requests from Regulatory Agencies; SLI Phase enrollment was completed for the planned participants. In addition, this amendment is to update the end of study definition and the section regarding continued access to study intervention after the end of study.

versions.rationale: To evaluate vepdegestrant (ARV-471) plus palbociclib versus letrozole plus palbociclib in participants with estrogen receptor-positive, HER2-negative advanced breast cancer.

population.name: Main Population
population.label: Study Population

notes.text: includesHealthySubjects inferred as false because this is a disease-specific oncology study.

population.description:
Approximately 50 and 1130 participants will be randomized in SLI and Phase 3, respectively.

Note: "Enrolled" means a participant's agreement to participate in a clinical study following completion of the informed consent process and randomization to study intervention.

The SLI Phase enrollment has been completed for the planned participants; the Phase 3 portion of the study will not be conducted. This is due to a Sponsor decision, and is not for safety, efficacy reasons or requests from Agencies.

SLI and Phase 3 will have the same Inclusion/Exclusion criteria, except for inclusion criterion on blood and tumor biopsy provision which applies to participants for Phase 3 only.

arms.name: Dose Level 1
arms.label: Dose Level 1
arms.description: Participants will receive: o Vepdegestrant as two 100 mg tablets (ie, 200 mg QD), orally, once daily, continuously, in a 28-day cycle, plus o Palbociclib as a 100 mg capsule, orally, once daily for 21 consecutive days followed by 7 days off treatment, in a 28-day cycle.

arms.name: Dose Level 2
arms.label: Dose Level 2
arms.description: Participants will receive:  Vepdegestrant as two 100 mg tablets (ie, 200 mg QD), orally, once daily, continuously, in a 28-day cycle, plus  Palbociclib as 75 mg capsule orally, once daily for 21 consecutive days followed by 7 days off treatment, in a 28-day cycle.

studyDesigns.name: Randomized Open-Label Parallel-Group Design
studyDesigns.label: This is an international Phase 3 randomized, open-label, parallel-group, multicenter clinical trial aimed to demonstrate that vepdegestrant in combination with palbociclib is superior to letrozole in combination with palbociclib in prolonging the PFS by BICR assessment in female and male participants with ER(+)/HER2(-) aBC who have not received any prior systemic anti-cancer treatment for their advanced disease.

epochs.name: Screening
epochs.label: Screening
epochs.description: The screening period will occur within 28 days prior to randomization. Screening procedures should be completed within 28 days from randomization unless otherwise specified.

epochs.name: Active Treatment Phase
epochs.label: Active Treatment Phase
epochs.description: Study intervention will be administered in 28-day cycles. Participants will continue to receive assigned study intervention until objective disease progression by RECIST v1.1, unacceptable toxicity, death, participant refused further treatment, or one of the other reasons for treatment discontinuation as specified in the protocol is met. There is a maximum of 60 days between visits during the Active Treatment Phase.

epochs.name: Safety Follow-Up
epochs.label: Safety Follow-Up
epochs.description: At least 28 calendar days and no more than 35 calendar days after discontinuation of study intervention or prior to initiation of any new anticancer therapy whichever comes first, participants will return to site to undergo the safety FU assessments as reported in the SoA.

epochs.name: Long Term FU
epochs.label: Long Term Follow-up
epochs.description: Long-Term Follow-up: Contraception for male participants and the start date of the first FU anticancer therapy (if not already collected during the Safety FU) will be collected as per SoA. In LTFU, tumor assessment (including bone scan, if applicable) is required only for participants who discontinued the study treatment for reason other than PD. Tumor assessment (including bone scan, if applicable), with the frequency reported in the SoA, should continue to be performed and reported in the CRF until PD.

elements.name: EL1
elements.label: Informed Consent
elements.description: The investigator or qualified designee will explain the nature of the study, including the risks and benefits, to the participant and answer all questions regarding the study. The participant should be given sufficient time and opportunity to ask questions and to decide whether or not to participate in the trial. Participants must be informed that their participation is voluntary. Participants (if allowed by local regulations) will be required to sign a statement of informed consent that meets the requirements of 21 CFR 50, local regulations, ICH GCP guidelines, privacy and data protection requirements, where applicable, and the IRB/EC or study center. The medical record must include a statement that written informed consent was obtained before the participant was enrolled in the study and the date on which the written consent was obtained.

transitionEndRule.name: Consent completed prior to assessments
transitionEndRule.text: ICD must be obtained 28 days from randomization and prior to any protocol required assessments being performed, unless otherwise specified eg, assessments by imaging (see Section 8.2.1).

transitionStartRule.name: Informed consent obtained
transitionStartRule.text: ICD must be obtained 28 days from randomization and prior to any protocol required assessments being performed, unless otherwise specified eg, assessments by imaging (see Section 8.2.1).

elements.name: EL2
elements.label: Screen for Inclusion/Exclusion Criteria
elements.description: Screening procedures should be completed within 28 days from randomization unless otherwise specified. See Section 5.1 and Section 5.2.

transitionEndRule.name: Screening completion
transitionEndRule.text: Screening procedures should be completed within 28 days from randomization unless otherwise specified.

transitionStartRule.name: Screening start window
transitionStartRule.text: Screening: screening procedures should be completed within 28 days from randomization unless otherwise specified.

elements.name: EL3
elements.label: Randomization
elements.description: Randomization must occur after having reviewed all eligibility criteria and confirmed they are satisfied. At randomization, randomization number and dose level are assigned. Study treatment must start 3 days from randomization.

transitionEndRule.name: Start of study treatment window
transitionEndRule.text: Study treatment must start 3 days from randomization.

transitionStartRule.name: Randomization occurs after eligibility review
transitionStartRule.text: Randomization must occur after having reviewed all eligibility criteria and confirmed they are satisfied.

elements.name: EL4
elements.label: vepdegestrant administration
elements.description: vepdgestrant is given orally with food in continuous daily dosing for a 28-day cycle. See Section 6.1.1 and Section 6.1.1.1 for details on drug administration.

elements.name: EL5
elements.label: Palbociclib administration
elements.description: Palbociclib is given orally with food in daily dosing from Day 1 to Day 21 (21 days) followed by 7 days off-treatment, for a 28-day cycle. Palbociclib drug intake should occur at the same time of vepdgestrant.

transitionStartRule.name: Palbociclib dosing schedule
transitionStartRule.text: Once Daily on Days 1-21 followed by 7 days off

elements.name: EL6
elements.label: Drug Compliance
elements.description: When participants self-administer study interventions at home, compliance with study interventions will be assessed on Day 1 of each cycle, prior to the dispensing of study interventions for the next cycle. After implementation of Amendment 3, compliance will be assessed every other cycle, prior to the dispensing of study interventions for the next two cycles. Participants will receive a dosing diary to document self-administered dosing of study intervention in each cycle to include the dose of study intervention taken, the date of dosing, if any doses were missed and the reason for the missed dose.

transitionEndRule.name: Compliance assessment action
transitionEndRule.text: prior to dispensing study interventions for the following cycle.

transitionStartRule.name: Compliance assessment timing
transitionStartRule.text: From Cycle 2 onward, compliance will be performed on Day 1 of each cycle, prior to dispensing study interventions for the following cycle.

elements.name: EL7
elements.label: Adverse Event Reporting
elements.description: Any observed AEs must be reported in the eCRF. See Section 8.4.1 and Appendix 3 for details on AE/SAE reporting requirements.

transitionStartRule.name: AE reporting requirement
transitionStartRule.text: Any observed AEs must be reported in the eCRF.

elements.name: EL8
elements.label: Safety Follow-Up Visit
elements.description: At least 28 calendar days and no more than 35 calendar days after discontinuation of study intervention or prior to initiation of any new anticancer therapy whichever comes first, participants will return to site to undergo the safety FU assessments as reported in the SoA. Any SAEs occurring during the 28-day from last dose (active reporting period) must still be reported to Pfizer Safety irrespective of any intervening treatment.

transitionEndRule.name: Safety FU minimum duration
transitionEndRule.text: Safety FU is a minimum of 28 days from last dose OR prior to initiation of a new anticancer therapy, whichever comes first (See Section 7.1.1.1).

transitionStartRule.name: Safety FU timing
transitionStartRule.text: At least 28 calendar days and no more than 35 calendar days after discontinuation of study intervention or prior to initiation of any new anticancer therapy whichever comes first, participants will return to site to undergo the safety FU assessments as reported in the SoA.

elements.name: EL9
elements.label: Long-Term Follow-up (SLI only)
elements.description: Contraception for male participants and the start date of the first FU anticancer therapy (if not already collected during the Safety FU) will be collected as per SoA. Of Note: in LTFU, tumor assessment (including bone scan, if applicable) is required only for participants who discontinued the study treatment for reason other than PD. Tumor assessment (including bone scan, if applicable), with the frequency reported in the SoA, should continue to be performed and reported in the CRF until PD.

estimands.name: SLI Estimand Grade4 Neutropenia
estimands.description: Intercurrent events for Grade 4 neutropenia AE: All neutropenia AEs reported by investigator with onset within the SLI observation period will be considered. This includes neutropenia AEs with onsets during study treatment or after treatment discontinuation occurring during the SLI observation period.
estimands.populationSummary: Participants enrolled in the Study Lead-in (SLI) included in the SLI Safety Analysis Set (SLI SAS).

intercurrentEvents.name: Neutropenia_onset_within_SLI_period
intercurrentEvents.text: All neutropenia AEs reported by investigator with onset within the SLI observation period will be considered. This includes neutropenia AEs with onsets during study treatment or after treatment discontinuation occurring during the SLI observation period.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: All neutropenia AEs reported by investigator with onset within the SLI observation period will be considered.

estimands.name: SLI Estimand Dose Reduction/Discontinuation
estimands.description: No intercurrent events for dose reduction and drug discontinuation.
estimands.populationSummary: Participants enrolled in the Study Lead-in (SLI) included in the SLI Safety Analysis Set (SLI SAS).

intercurrentEvents.name: DoseReductionOrDiscontinuation_no_intercurrent_events
intercurrentEvents.text: No intercurrent events for dose reduction and drug discontinuation.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: No intercurrent events for dose reduction and drug discontinuation.

estimands.name: Primary Estimand PFS
estimands.description:
The estimand is defined by the following attributes:

 Population: Participants with ER(+), HER2(-) breast cancer who have not received any prior systemic anti-cancer treatment for advanced disease and have been randomized in the Phase 3.

 Endpoint: PFS, defined as the time from the date of randomization to the date of first documented disease progression, as determined by BICR assessment per RECIST v1.1, or death due to any cause, whichever occurs first.

 Intercurrent events: PFS data will be censored on the date of the last adequate tumor assessment for participants with the following intercurrent events:

o Discontinue the study treatment due to withdrawal of consent prior to an event

o Start a new anti-cancer therapy prior to an event

o Have an event after an unacceptably long interval (2 or more missing or nonevaluable assessments)

o Lost to follow-up

 Censoring: See Table 12.

 Population-level summary: Hazard ratio for PFS including all randomized participants.
estimands.populationSummary: All participants randomized in Phase 3 with ER(+)/HER2(-) advanced breast cancer who are treatment-naïve for advanced disease.

intercurrentEvents.name: WithdrawConsent_or_NewTherapy_or_MissingAssessments_or_LostToFollowUp
intercurrentEvents.text: Discontinue the study treatment due to withdrawal of consent prior to an event; Start a new anti-cancer therapy prior to an event; Have an event after an unacceptably long interval (2 or more missing or nonevaluable assessments); Lost to follow-up.
intercurrentEvents.strategy: PFS data will be censored on the date of the last adequate tumor assessment for participants with the listed intercurrent events.

estimands.name: Key Secondary Estimand OS
estimands.description:
The estimand is defined by the following attributes:

 Population: Patients with ER(+), HER2(-) breast cancer who have not received any prior systemic anti-cancer treatment for advanced disease and have been randomized in the Phase 3.

 Endpoint: OS, defined as the time from the date of randomization to the date of death due to any cause.

 Intercurrent events: All data will be used regardless of the occurrence of intercurrent events (discontinuation of study treatment, use of subsequent treatments, discontinuation of study, etc.).

 Censoring: Participants last known to be alive are censored at date of last contact.

 Population-level summary: Hazard ratio for OS including all randomized participants.
estimands.populationSummary: All participants randomized in Phase 3 with ER(+)/HER2(-) advanced breast cancer who are treatment-naïve for advanced disease.

intercurrentEvents.name: OS_all_data_included
intercurrentEvents.text: All data will be used regardless of the occurrence of intercurrent events (discontinuation of study treatment, use of subsequent treatments, discontinuation of study, etc.).
intercurrentEvents.strategy: All data will be used regardless of the occurrence of intercurrent events.

studyDesigns.rationale: The purpose of this study is to demonstrate that vepdegestrant in combination with palbociclib provides superior clinical benefit compared to letrozole in combination with palbociclib in participants with ER(+)/HER2(-) aBC who have not received any prior systemic anti-cancer therapies for their advanced disease.

objectives.name: OBJ_PR_Phase3_PFS
objectives.text: To demonstrate whether vepdegestrant plus palbociclib prolongs progression-free survival versus letrozole plus palbociclib in participants with ER(+)/HER2(-) advanced breast cancer who are treatment-naïve for advanced disease.

endpoints.name: EP_PR_PFS_BICR
endpoints.text: Progression-free survival (PFS) by blinded independent central review (BICR) per RECIST v1.1, measured from randomization until first documented progression or death.
endpoints.description: Progression-free survival (PFS), defined as the time from the date of randomization to the date of first documented disease progression, as determined by BICR assessment per RECIST v1.1, or death due to any cause, whichever occurs first.

objectives.description: To demonstrate that vepdegestrant in combination with palbociclib is superior to letrozole plus palbociclib in prolonging progression free survival (PFS) in participants with ER(+)/HER2(-) aBC who have not received any prior systemic anticancer therapy for their advanced disease.

objectives.name: OBJ_PR_SLI_RP3D
objectives.text: To determine the recommended Phase 3 dose (RP3D) of palbociclib when combined with vepdegestrant in the Study Lead-in (SLI).

endpoints.name: EP_PR_SLI_Grade4Neutropenia
endpoints.text: Incidence of Grade 4 neutropenia AEs (NCI CTCAE v5.0) with onset during the first four cycles in SLI participants.
endpoints.description: Incidence of Grade 4 neutropenia AE (as graded by NCI CTCAE v5.0), with onset within the first 4 cycles.

endpoints.name: EP_PR_SLI_DoseReductionOrDiscontinuation
endpoints.text: Incidence of dose reduction or drug discontinuation of any study drug occurring within the first four cycles in SLI participants.
endpoints.description: Incidence of dose reduction or drug discontinuation within the first 4 cycles.

objectives.description: To determine the RP3D of palbociclib when given in combination with vepdegestrant.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_Phase3_OS
objectives.text: To demonstrate whether vepdegestrant plus palbociclib prolongs overall survival versus letrozole plus palbociclib in the Phase 3 randomized population.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_Phase3_OS
endpoints.text: Overall survival (OS) defined as time from randomization to death from any cause in the randomized Phase 3 population.
endpoints.description: Overall Survival (OS), defined as the time from the date of randomization to the date of death due to any cause.

objectives.description: To demonstrate that vepdegestrant in combination with palbociclib is superior to letrozole in combination with palbociclib, in prolonging overall survival (OS).

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_SLI_Safety
objectives.text: To evaluate safety and tolerability of both palbociclib dose levels given with vepdegestrant in the SLI.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_SLI_AEs_SAEs
endpoints.text: Incidence of adverse events (AEs) and serious adverse events (SAEs) in SLI participants.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_SLI_LabAbnormalities
endpoints.text: Incidence of clinically significant laboratory abnormalities in SLI participants.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_SLI_ECGAbnormalities
endpoints.text: Incidence of ECG abnormalities in SLI participants.

objectives.description: To evaluate safety and tolerability of both dose levels.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_TumorControl_SLI
objectives.text: To evaluate tumor control measures and duration of response in SLI participants across dose levels.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_SLI_ORR_Investigator
endpoints.text: Objective Response Rate (ORR) by investigator assessment per RECIST v1.1, confirmed CR or PR.
endpoints.description: Objective Response (OR: confirmed CR or PR) by investigator assessment per RECIST v1.1.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_SLI_CBR_Investigator
endpoints.text: Clinical Benefit Response (CBR) by investigator per RECIST v1.1 (confirmed CR/PR at any time or SD/non-CR non-PD ≥24 weeks).
endpoints.description: Clinical Benefit Response (CBR: confirmed CR or PR at any time or SD or non- CR/non-PD ≥24 weeks) by investigator assessment per RECIST v 1.1.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_SLI_DoR_Investigator
endpoints.text: Duration of Response (DoR) measured from first documentation of CR/PR to first documentation of PD or death, by investigator assessment per RECIST v1.1.
endpoints.description: Duration of Response, defined as the time from the first documentation of CR or PR to the date of the first documentation of PD, as determined by investigator assessment per RECIST v1.1, or death due to any cause, whichever occurs first.

objectives.description: To evaluate measures of tumor control and duration of response in both dose levels.

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_PK_SLI
objectives.text: To evaluate plasma concentrations of vepdegestrant (and ARV-473) and palbociclib in SLI participants.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_SLI_PlasmaVepdegestrant_ARV473
endpoints.text: Plasma concentrations of vepdegestrant and its epimer ARV-473.
endpoints.description: Plasma concentrations of vepdegestrant and ARV-473.

objectives.description: To evaluate the concentrations of vepdegestrant and ARV-473; To evaluate the concentrations of palbociclib.

objectives.name: OBJ_EXPD_Biomarker_PDK
objectives.text: To explore correlation between biomarkers (eg, TK1 or ctDNA) and exposure/clinical outcomes in relevant participants.

endpoints.name: EP_EXPD_TK1_PDK
endpoints.text: Measurements of surrogate pharmacodynamic markers involved in cell proliferation (eg, TK1) and their correlation with exposure.
endpoints.description: Measurements of surrogate pharmacodynamic markers involved in cell proliferation (eg, TK1).

endpoints.name: EP_EXPD_ctDNA_Phase3
endpoints.text: ctDNA quantitative changes from pre-treatment and their association with clinical outcomes (Phase 3 biomarker endpoint).
endpoints.description: ctDNA plasma quantitative changes from pre-treatment to evaluate potential predictability of their associations with clinical outcomes.

objectives.description: To evaluate correlation of biomarker with exposure of vepdegestrant in combination with palbociclib and other clinical outcomes

objectives.name: OBJ_SC_Phase3_Secondary_Efficacy
objectives.text: To compare measures of tumor control and duration of response between Phase 3 treatment arms and evaluate PROs and biomarker endpoints.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_Phase3_ORR_BICR
endpoints.text: Objective response (confirmed CR or PR) by BICR assessment per RECIST v1.1.
endpoints.description: Objective Response (OR: confirmed CR or PR) by BICR assessment per RECIST v1.1.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_Phase3_CBR_BICR
endpoints.text: Clinical benefit response (CBR) by BICR per RECIST v1.1 (confirmed CR/PR or SD/non-CR non-PD ≥24 weeks).
endpoints.description: Clinical Benefit Response (CBR: confirmed CR or PR at any time or SD or non- CR/non-PD ≥24 weeks) by BICR assessment per RECIST v 1.1.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_Phase3_DoR_BICR
endpoints.text: Duration of response by BICR per RECIST v1.1, from first documentation of CR/PR to PD or death.
endpoints.description: Duration of Response, defined as the time from the first documentation of CR or PR to the date of the first documentation of PD, as determined by BICR assessment per RECIST v1.1, or death due to any cause, whichever occurs first.

endpoints.name: EP_SC_Phase3_PROs
endpoints.text: Patient-reported outcomes using EQ-5D-5L, EORTC QLQ-C30, and EORTC QLQ-BR23 to evaluate quality of life and functioning.
endpoints.description: EuroQol EQ-5D-5L; EORTC QLQ-C30; EORTC QLQ-BR23

objectives.description: To compare measures of tumor control between treatment arms and evaluate duration of response within each treatment arm.

indications.name: IND1
indications.label: Estrogen receptor-positive (ER+), HER2-negative advanced breast cancer
indications.description: Estrogen receptor-positive (ER+), HER2-negative advanced (metastatic or unresectable) breast cancer; participants who have not received prior systemic anti-cancer treatment for advanced disease

eligibilityCriteria.name:
Age and Sex:
Participants aged 18 years or older (or the min
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Age and Sex:
Participants aged 18 years or older (or the minimum age of consent in accordance with local regulations) at screening.

Female participants under 60 years of age, with cessation of regular menses for 12 consecutive months and with no other alternative medical cause, must have a FSH level within the post-menopausal level, as per local laboratory reference range.

Pre-/peri-menopausal female participants and male participants must agree to initiate or continue an LHRH agonist as per SoA and Section 6.9.1

IOCBP participants and male participants must agree to use contraception. Refer to Appendix 4 for further details.

Male participants are eligible to participate if they agree to the following requirements during the intervention period and for at least 97 days after the last dose of study intervention, which corresponds to the time needed to eliminate reproductive safety risk of the study intervention(s) plus an additional 90 days (a spermatogenesis cycle):

 Refrain from donating sperm for at least 97 days after last dose of study intervention.

PLUS either:

 Be abstinent from heterosexual or homosexual intercourse as their preferred and usual lifestyle (abstinent on a long-term and persistent basis) and agree to remain abstinent.

OR

 Must agree to use a male condom when engaging in any activity that allows for passage of ejaculate to another person.

In addition to male condom use, a highly effective method of contraception may be considered in WOCBP partners of male participants.

The criteria below are part of Inclusion Criterion #1 (Age and Sex; Section 5.1) and specify the reproductive requirements for including female participants. Refer to Section 10.4.4 for a complete list of contraceptive methods permitted in the study.

A participant is eligible to participate if the participant (a) is not pregnant or breastfeeding; and (b) at least 1 of the following conditions applies:

 Is not an IOCBP (see definition in Section 10.4.3).

 Is an IOCBP and agrees to use a contraceptive method that is highly effective (failure rate of <1% per year) with low user dependency during the intervention period and for at least 21 days after the last dose of study intervention, which corresponds to the time needed to eliminate any reproductive safety risk of the study intervention(s). The investigator should confirm the use of the appropriate contraceptive method in relationship to the first dose of study intervention.

OR

 Is an IOCBP and agrees to use a highly effective (failure rate of <1% per year) userdependent method of contraception during the intervention period and for at least 21 days after the last dose of study intervention, which corresponds to the time needed to eliminate any reproductive safety risk of the study intervention(s). The investigator should confirm the use of the appropriate contraceptive method in relationship to the first dose of study intervention.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants who are willing and able, to comply with all schedul
eligibilityCriteria.text: Participants who are willing and able, to comply with all scheduled visits, treatment plans, laboratory tests, lifestyle considerations, and other study procedures.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Phase 3 ONLY. Participants must provide a blood sample AND a t
eligibilityCriteria.text: Phase 3 ONLY. Participants must provide a blood sample AND a tumor sample collected at the time of diagnosis of locally advanced/metastatic disease. If not available, a de novo biopsy is required. The sole exception is those patients with bone only disease for whom archival tumor sample at initial diagnosis is acceptable. Refer to Section 8.7 for further details.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Histological or cytological confirmation of BC with evidence o
eligibilityCriteria.text: Histological or cytological confirmation of BC with evidence of locoregionally advanced or metastatic disease, not amenable to surgical resection or radiation therapy with curative intent.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Documented ER(+), defined as ER(+) ≥1% stained cells by an a
eligibilityCriteria.text: Documented ER(+), defined as ER(+) ≥1% stained cells by an assay consistent with local standards, on the most recent tumor biopsy, ie, at diagnosis of recurrence or metastatic disease (Allison et al, 2020). The sole exception is those patients with bone only disease for whom ER(+) using archival tissue at initial diagnosis is acceptable.

eligibilityCriteria.name:
Other Inclusion Criteria:
Eastern Cooperative Oncology Gro
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Other Inclusion Criteria:
Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) performance status (PS) ≤2 (see Appendix 13 Section 10.13.2).

eligibilityCriteria.name: Documented HER2(-) tumor by either IHC or in-situ hybridizatio
eligibilityCriteria.text: Documented HER2(-) tumor by either IHC or in-situ hybridization per ASCO/CAP guideline (Wolff et al, 2018) on the most recent tumor biopsy, as per above.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants who have bilateral BCs which are both ER(+)/HER2(
eligibilityCriteria.text: Participants who have bilateral BCs which are both ER(+)/HER2(-) are eligible.

eligibilityCriteria.name: No prior systemic anti-cancer therapy for their locoregiona
eligibilityCriteria.text: No prior systemic anti-cancer therapy for their locoregionally advanced or metastatic disease.

eligibilityCriteria.name: At least 1 measurable lesion as defined by RECIST v1.1. Bone
eligibilityCriteria.text: At least 1 measurable lesion as defined by RECIST v1.1. Bone only disease: participants with only non-measurable lesions are eligible. Refer to Section 8.2.1 and Appendix 11 for further details.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Other medical (eg, hypersensitivity) or psychiatric condition
eligibilityCriteria.text: Other medical (eg, hypersensitivity) or psychiatric condition including recent (within the past year) or active suicidal ideation/behavior or laboratory abnormality that may increase the risk of study participation or, in the investigator's judgment, make the participant inappropriate for the study.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Any other solid tumors within the past 3 years, except for th
eligibilityCriteria.text: Any other solid tumors within the past 3 years, except for the following: 1) adequately treated basal or squamous cell carcinoma of the skin; and 2) curatively treated in situ carcinoma of the cervix. For all other solid tumors, they must have been curatively treated and with no evidence of disease for >3 years. Participants with inflammatory BC, prior hematopoietic stem cell or bone marrow transplantation are excluded.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants with newly diagnosed brain metastasis or symptoma
eligibilityCriteria.text: Participants with newly diagnosed brain metastasis or symptomatic central nervous system (CNS) metastases, carcinomatous meningitis, or leptomeningeal disease as indicated by clinical symptoms, cerebral edema, and/or progressive growth. Participants with a history of CNS metastases or cord compression are eligible if they have been definitively treated (eg, radiotherapy, stereotactic surgery), are clinically stable (including participants with residual CNS symptoms/deficits) and off enzymeinducing anticonvulsants and steroids for at least 14 days prior to randomization.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Participants in visceral crisis at risk of immediately life-
eligibilityCriteria.text: Participants in visceral crisis at risk of immediately life-threatening complications in the short term, including participants with massive uncontrolled effusions (pleural, pericardial & peritoneal), pulmonary lymphangitis, or liver involvement >50%.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Impaired cardiovascular function or clinically significant c
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Impaired cardiovascular function or clinically significant cardiovascular diseases, defined as:

Symptomatic cardiac valve disease. Participants with mitral valve prolapse that is asymptomatic or not associated with clinically significant sequalae (eg, mitral regurgitation) are eligible.

Any of the following in the previous 6 months: myocardial infarction, severe/unstable angina, coronary/peripheral artery bypass graft, symptomatic congestive heart failure (New York Heart Association Class III or IV), cerebrovascular accident, transient ischemic attack, or symptomatic pulmonary embolism or other clinically significant episode of thromboembolic disease, congenital long QT syndrome, Torsade de Pointes, clinical important arrhythmias, left anterior hemiblock (bifascicular block), ongoing cardiac dysrhythmias of NCI CTCAE Grade ≥2, atrial fibrillation of any grade.

Cardiac rhythm device/pacemaker. Participants with cardiac rhythm device/pacemaker must be discussed in detail with sponsor medical monitor to determine eligibility.

QTcF >470 msec on screening ECG.

eligibilityCriteria.name:
Prior/Concomitant Therapy:

Prior treatments:

Primary endocri
eligibilityCriteria.text:
Prior/Concomitant Therapy:

Prior treatments:

Primary endocrine resistance: relapse while on the first 2 years of standard adjuvant ET.

Secondary endocrine resistance:

o relapse while on standard adjuvant ET but after the first 2 years

o relapse within 12 months of completing standard adjuvant ET

o CDK4/6i, vepdegestrant, fulvestrant, elacestrant and other investigational agents (including novel ET, any SERDs, SERCAs, CERANs) in any setting.

Participation in other studies involving investigational drug(s) within 28 days prior to randomization. If in the FU Phase, the participant is eligible provided at least 5 half-lives have elapsed from the last dose.

Major surgery, radiotherapy, or systemic anticancer therapy within 14 days from randomization (28 days or 5 half-lives, whichever is shorter, for anticancer therapy containing an antibody-based agents, approved or investigational). Participants who received prior radiotherapy to ≥25% of bone marrow are not eligible independent of when it was received (see Appendix 12: Bone Marrow Reserve in Adults).

eligibilityCriteria.name:
Diagnostic Assessments:
Hematologic abnormalities defined as:
Renal impairment defined as an eGFR <45 mL/min/1.73m 2 calcu
eligibilityCriteria.text: Renal impairment defined as an eGFR <45 mL/min/1.73m 2 calculated using the 2021 CKD-EPI equations, as outlined in Appendix 7.

eligibilityCriteria.name:
Hepatic dysfunction defined as:

Total bilirubin >1.5 x ULN (≥3
Known active infection including SARS-CoV-2, HBV, HCV, and HIV
eligibilityCriteria.text: Known active infection including SARS-CoV-2, HBV, HCV, and HIV or AIDS-related illness (screening for chronic conditions is not required).

eligibilityCriteria.name: Refractory nausea and vomiting, chronic gastrointestinal (GI)
eligibilityCriteria.text: Refractory nausea and vomiting, chronic gastrointestinal (GI) disease, GI ulcer, GI bleeding, inability to swallow the formulated product or previous significant gastric (total or partial) or bowel resection that would preclude adequate absorption of study interventions.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Any unresolved toxicities from prior surgeries or therapies G
eligibilityCriteria.text: Any unresolved toxicities from prior surgeries or therapies Grade >1 (Grade >2 for peripheral neuropathy) by NCI CTCAE v5.0 at the time of randomization, except for alopecia.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Concurrent administration of medications, food or herbal sup
eligibilityCriteria.text: Concurrent administration of medications, food or herbal supplements that are strong inhibitors or strong inducers of CYP3A. Prior use of strong CYP3A inhibitors must be stopped 7 days before randomization and strong CYP3A inducers must be stopped 14 days before randomization. Refer to Section 6.9 (Prior and Concomitant Therapy) and Appendix 9 for the list of prohibited drugs.

eligibilityCriteria.name: Investigator site staff directly involved in the conduct of t
eligibilityCriteria.text: Investigator site staff directly involved in the conduct of the study and their family members, site staff otherwise supervised by the investigator, and sponsor and sponsor delegate employees directly involved in the conduct of the study and their family members.

studyInterventions.name: Palbociclib
studyInterventions.description: CDK4/6 inhibitor administered in combination with vepdegestrant (SLI) or with letrozole (Phase 3 control arm); provided in capsule form at multiple strengths; schedule is once daily on Days 1-21 followed by 7 days off in a 28-day cycle.

administrations.name: Palbociclib 100 mg QD Days 1-21, then 7 days off (28-day cycle)

duration.text: Once daily on Days 1-21 followed by 7 days off each 28-day cycle; continue until objective disease progression, unacceptable toxicity, death, participant refusal, or other protocol-specified reasons.
duration.reasonDurationWillVary: Participants will continue to receive assigned study intervention until objective disease progression by RECIST v1.1, unacceptable toxicity, death, participant refused further treatment, or one of the other reasons for treatment discontinuation as specified in the protocol is met.

administrations.description: Administer orally with food; once daily on Days 1-21 followed by 7 days off in each 28-day cycle.

administrations.name: Palbociclib 75 mg QD Days 1-21, then 7 days off (28-day cycle)

duration.text: Once daily on Days 1-21 followed by 7 days off each 28-day cycle; continue until objective disease progression, unacceptable toxicity, death, participant refusal, or other protocol-specified reasons.
duration.reasonDurationWillVary: Participants will continue to receive assigned study intervention until objective disease progression by RECIST v1.1, unacceptable toxicity, death, participant refused further treatment, or one of the other reasons for treatment discontinuation as specified in the protocol is met.

administrations.description: Administer orally with food; once daily on Days 1-21 followed by 7 days off in each 28-day cycle.

administrations.name: Palbociclib 125 mg QD Days 1-21, then 7 days off (28-day cycle)

duration.text: Once daily on Days 1-21 followed by 7 days off each 28-day cycle; continue until objective disease progression, unacceptable toxicity, death, participant refusal, or other protocol-specified reasons.
duration.reasonDurationWillVary: Participants will continue to receive assigned study intervention until objective disease progression by RECIST v1.1, unacceptable toxicity, death, participant refused further treatment, or one of the other reasons for treatment discontinuation as specified in the protocol is met.

administrations.description: Administer orally with food; once daily on Days 1-21 followed by 7 days off in each 28-day cycle. Used in Phase 3 control arm per protocol.

administrations.name: Palbociclib 50 mg QD Days 1-21, then 7 days off (28-day cycle)

duration.text: Once daily on Days 1-21 followed by 7 days off each 28-day cycle; continue until objective disease progression, unacceptable toxicity, death, participant refusal, or other protocol-specified reasons.
duration.reasonDurationWillVary: Participants will continue to receive assigned study intervention until objective disease progression by RECIST v1.1, unacceptable toxicity, death, participant refused further treatment, or one of the other reasons for treatment discontinuation as specified in the protocol is met.

administrations.description: Dose reduction allowed per protocol (two 25 mg capsules once daily on Days 1-21 followed by 7 days off). Administer orally with food.

administrableProducts.name: Palbociclib capsule (IBRANCE)
administrableProducts.description: Capsule; multiple unit strengths provided per protocol (25 mg, 75 mg, 100 mg, 125 mg).

substance.name: palbociclib
substance.description: Oral selective cyclin-dependent kinase 4 and 6 (CDK4/6) inhibitor.

studyInterventions.name: LHRH agonist (eg, goserelin or leuprolide acetate)
studyInterventions.description: Ovarian/testicular suppression therapy for pre-/peri-menopausal females (and males) to be administered starting on Cycle 1 Day 1 and continued as specified; examples include goserelin or leuprolide acetate.

studyInterventions.name: Letrozole
studyInterventions.description: Nonsteroidal aromatase inhibitor used as active comparator in Phase 3 control arm (Arm B); administered 2.5 mg once daily.

administrations.name: Letrozole 2.5 mg QD

duration.text: Once daily on Days 1-28 of each 28-day cycle; continue until objective disease progression, unacceptable toxicity, death, participant refusal, or other protocol-specified reasons.
duration.reasonDurationWillVary: Participants will continue to receive assigned study intervention until objective disease progression by RECIST v1.1, unacceptable toxicity, death, participant refused further treatment, or one of the other reasons for treatment discontinuation as specified in the protocol is met.

administrations.description: Administer orally with food; once daily on Days 1-28 of each 28-day cycle. Dose reduction is NOT permitted.

substance.name: letrozole
substance.description: Nonsteroidal aromatase inhibitor that decreases estrogen production.

studyInterventions.name: Vepdegestrant (ARV-471)
studyInterventions.description: Investigational oral ER degrader (PROTAC) administered in combination with palbociclib in the SLI and Phase 3 Arm A; primary study agent under investigation.

administrations.name: Vepdegestrant 200 mg QD

duration.text: Once daily on Days 1-28 of each 28-day cycle; continue until objective disease progression, unacceptable toxicity, death, participant refusal, or other protocol-specified reasons.
duration.reasonDurationWillVary: Participants will continue to receive assigned study intervention until objective disease progression by RECIST v1.1, unacceptable toxicity, death, participant refused further treatment, or one of the other reasons for treatment discontinuation as specified in the protocol is met.

administrations.description: Take orally with food; preferably in the morning. Participants instructed to take with food and at approximately the same time each day.

administrations.name: Vepdegestrant 100 mg QD (dose reduction)

duration.text: Once daily on Days 1-28 of each 28-day cycle; continue until objective disease progression, unacceptable toxicity, death, participant refusal, or other protocol-specified reasons.
duration.reasonDurationWillVary: Participants will continue to receive assigned study intervention until objective disease progression by RECIST v1.1, unacceptable toxicity, death, participant refused further treatment, or one of the other reasons for treatment discontinuation as specified in the protocol is met.

administrations.description: Take orally with food; dose reduction level per protocol (one 100 mg tablet once daily).

substance.name: vepdegestrant
substance.description: Hetero-bifunctional PROTAC that binds the estrogen receptor (ER) and cereblon E3 ligase to induce ER ubiquitination and proteasomal degradation.
